---
title: "Pathological features"
format: html
---

| Ultrasound finding | Pneumothorax | Pleural effusion | Pulmonary edema | Pneumonia |
|:---|:--:|:--:|:--:|:--:|
| A-lines | — | — | — | — |
| B-lines[^pathological-features-1] | ⊘ | — | \+ | \+ |
| Lung sliding | ⊘ | — | — | — |
| Lung pulse | ⊘ | — | — | — |
| Lung point | ✓ | — | — | — |
| Spine sign[^pathological-features-2] | — | ✓ | ++ | \+ |
| Lung condensation | — | ++ | \+ | ++ |

: Diagnostic contribution of common lung ultrasound findings.\
—: no diagnostic value. ⊘: rules out. ✓: rules in. +/++ in favor/strongly in favor.

[^pathological-features-1]: B-lines in pulmonary edema are usually approximately symetric and declive, whether assymetric focal in pneumonia.

[^pathological-features-2]: Pleural effusion is more frequent in pulmonary edema than in pneumonia, and is usually approximately symetrical in pulmonary edema, and assymetrical in pneumonia.
